branch_answers.answer_text: Influencer B would sway me more. With 2M followers, it's clearly popular and the fragrant focus aligns with what I love—a good smell that boosts my confidence. Lifestyle creators feel relatable, and their endorsements make products seem enjoyable and trusted by many.
branch_answers.concept_test_prediction: Would favor concepts highlighting aromatic experiences, mood enhancement, and personal stories (e.g., 'feeling refreshed with Indian floral scents'). Rational claims might be secondary unless woven into an engaging narrative.
branch_answers.ad_test_prediction: Responds well to ads with emotional narratives, user testimonials, and visually appealing content that evokes feelings (e.g., scenes of confidence or relaxation). Clinical ads might feel impersonal and less engaging.

branch_answers.question_text: A body wash brand you've never tried sends you a ₹150 discount coupon valid for 48 hours on their website. Their body wash costs ₹399 (so you'd pay ₹249). Your current body wash still has about 2 weeks of use left. Do you: A) Use the coupon now — great deal, I'll stock up B) Let it expire — I don't need body wash right now C) Browse their site but probably won't buy unless something really stands out
branch_answers.answer_text: I'd use the coupon right away—a ₹150 discount is too good to ignore, even if I don't need it immediately. It's like when I bought Moxie Beauty from an Instagram ad; a solid deal just pulls me in, and I can always stock up for later.
branch_answers.concept_test_prediction: This twin would show high immediate purchase intent for body wash concepts with limited-time discounts or flashy packaging, often skipping detailed evaluation of claims.
branch_answers.ad_test_prediction: Ads featuring urgency cues like '48-hour sale' or eye-catching visuals would drive quick clicks and conversions, with less need for in-depth information.

branch_answers.question_text: How does your current mood or activity influence your engagement with advertisements for personal care products like body wash? Describe when you are most and least likely to pay attention to such ads.
branch_answers.answer_text: I almost always ignore ads when I'm busy or stressed, like during study sessions. But if I'm scrolling through Instagram leisurely in the evening, I might stop and watch an ad if it catches my eye, especially if it's from a brand I recognize or has interesting claims. It really depends on what I'm doing at that moment.
branch_answers.concept_test_prediction: For a body wash concept presented during relaxed leisure time, this twin would be more open and positive, evaluating it based on fragrance and claims; during busy times, they would likely dismiss it or have negative perceptions.
branch_answers.ad_test_prediction: Ads shown in evening leisure scrolling on Instagram would have higher click-through rates and positive engagement; ads during work or study hours would be ignored or scrolled past without retention.

branch_answers.dimension_name: Aspirational vs Relatable Marketing Positioning Response
branch_answers.question_text: Imagine you're choosing between these three body washes at a store. Rank them in order of purchase preference: A) ₹149 — basic cleansing, no special claims, trusted old brand (like Lifebuoy) B) ₹299 — moisturizing, pleasant fragrance, mid-tier brand (like Nivea) C) ₹499 — organic ingredients, eco-friendly packaging, Instagram-famous D2C brand
branch_answers.answer_text: I'd rank B first, then C, then A. B offers moisturizing and a pleasant fragrance at a reasonable price, which matches what I usually go for. C is interesting as a D2C and organic option, but ₹499 is high unless it has proven benefits like long-lasting fragrance or clinical testing. A is too basic and doesn't address my skin concerns.
branch_answers.concept_test_prediction: Would prefer concepts that balance quality and price, with clear benefits like moisturizing or India-specific formulations. Luxury claims without substantiation may not appeal; instead, focus on practical advantages like fragrance longevity or skin compatibility.
branch_answers.ad_test_prediction: Ads showing real people in everyday scenarios, like a student using the product post-shower, would be effective. Content with ingredient breakdowns or before/after results (Q57) would build trust, while overly aspirational settings might seem disconnected.
